customManagers.description: Track the pnpm version pinned in scripts/Dockerfile

packageRules.description: Wait out pnpm 11's 24h minimumReleaseAge supply-chain policy so PRs don't arrive with failing installs
Bump pnpm together across package.json, workflows, and the Dockerfile
Group Go version and golangci-lint to avoid version mismatch
Group @connectrpc packages since connect-web pins an exact peer version of connect
Group i18next, react-i18next, and i18next plugins so peer-dep major bumps land together
React 19's renderer enforces an exact version match between react and react-dom at startup, so bump them together

v8's modern-browser detection imports a data: URI module, which our CSP script-src blocks — every browser then falls back to the broken legacy (SystemJS) bundle. Re-enable once the detection is CSP-compatible or we drop legacy chunks (renderLegacyChunks: false).

v10 dropped the browser-field mapping on the root export, so `import "vscode-languageclient"` loads the common build and the browser RAL is never installed — the LSP client dies with 'No runtime abstraction layer installed'. Re-enable after migrating imports to vscode-languageclient/browser and verifying LSP in a real browser.
